title: Choose activity type quickly
description: User picks from recent or categorized activity types to start recording without scrolling through long lists. Quick-type grid shows last-used activities first for faster selection.

title: Check GPS readiness before recording
description: User sees GPS signal quality before starting a GPS activity so they can wait for a fix. A 15-second timeout detects weak or absent signals and warns the user.

title: Recover from recording crash
description: User is prompted to resume or discard a previous recording after an app crash. Periodic 15-second backups ensure minimal data loss from unexpected termination.

title: Start paired workout recording
description: User can tap a planned calendar event to begin a recording pre-linked to that workout. The pairing flows through to the upload so intervals.icu matches it automatically.

title: Keep screen awake during recording
description: User's screen stays on while recording so they do not miss data or lose their session. The useKeepAwake hook prevents the OS from sleeping the display.

title: Auto-pause when stationary
description: User's recording automatically pauses when speed drops below a configurable threshold for several seconds. Eliminates inflated duration from traffic lights and rest stops.

title: View live recording metrics
description: User sees real-time speed, distance, heart rate, power, cadence, and elevation while recording. Data fields update continuously from GPS and sensor streams.

title: Monitor GPS signal quality
description: User sees colour-coded GPS accuracy indicator during recording so they know if tracking is reliable. Green (<5m), amber (5-15m), and red (>15m) thresholds provide clear feedback.

title: Lock screen during recording
description: User swipes to lock the recording screen to prevent accidental taps while exercising. The lock overlay shows elapsed time, distance, speed, and a mini map.

title: Pause and resume recording
description: User can pause, resume, lap, and stop recording with haptic-feedback controls. Long-press to stop prevents accidental activity termination.

title: See km split banners
description: User receives a brief banner notification at each kilometre split during recording. Provides real-time pacing feedback without needing to unlock the screen.

title: View heart rate zone bar
description: User sees a coloured bar indicating their current HR zone during recording. Zone colours match the athlete's configured HR zone boundaries.

title: Change activity type mid-recording
description: User can switch the activity type during or after recording without restarting. Useful when transitioning between sports in a multisport session.

title: Trim recording start and end
description: User adjusts dual-handle slider to trim unwanted GPS points before uploading. Removes warm-up walking or post-ride cooldown from the recorded activity.

title: Generate FIT file from recording
description: User's recording is encoded as a standard FIT file with GPS, HR, power, cadence, and lap data. Ensures compatibility with all fitness platforms and analysis tools.

title: Configure auto-pause thresholds
description: User sets per-sport speed thresholds for auto-pause in recording settings. Cycling, running, and walking each have independent sensitivity controls.

title: Select recording data fields
description: User chooses which metrics appear on the recording screen for GPS and indoor modes. Up to 15 different data field types can be toggled on or off.

title: Enter manual activity
description: User creates an indoor or equipment-free activity by entering duration, distance, and heart rate manually. Generates a valid upload without requiring GPS data.

title: Set RPE after recording
description: User rates perceived exertion on a 1-10 scale with colour-coded labels before uploading. Provides subjective effort context alongside objective metrics.

title: Backup recording periodically
description: Recording state is saved to the filesystem every 15 seconds during active recording. Crash recovery can restore the session from the most recent backup.

title: Queue uploads when offline
description: User's completed recordings are queued locally when there is no network connectivity. Files are stored on the filesystem and retried automatically when online.

title: Process upload queue automatically
description: Queued uploads are processed when the app returns to foreground or network reconnects. Failed uploads retry with exponential backoff up to 5 attempts before removal.

title: Upgrade OAuth permissions for upload
description: User is prompted to re-authenticate with write permissions when uploads fail with 403. A banner explains the issue and provides a one-tap grant-access flow.

title: Handle upload permission blocks
description: When an upload is blocked by insufficient OAuth scope, the entry is flagged rather than retried. All blocked entries are released after a successful permission upgrade.

title: Skip non-retriable upload errors
description: Uploads that fail with 4xx HTTP errors (except 408/429) are removed from the queue permanently. Prevents wasting bandwidth on requests that will never succeed.

title: View weekly summary card
description: User sees a hero metric (form, fitness, or fatigue) with sparkline trend on the home screen. Supporting metrics show weekly distance, TSS, and activity count at a glance.

title: Browse rotating insight headlines
description: User sees the top insight rotating every 8 seconds in the summary card corner. Tapping navigates to the Insights tab for the full detail view.

title: See today's training teaser
description: User sees a contextual headline about today's planned workout or typical activity pattern. Provides daily motivation grounded in actual training history.

title: Search and filter activities
description: User types in a search bar and applies sport-type chips to find specific activities in the feed. Keyboard dismisses on scroll for a smooth browsing experience.

title: Scroll infinite activity list
description: User scrolls through their entire activity history with infinite pagination loading more as needed. Pull-to-refresh triggers a fresh sync from the API.

title: Tap activity card to view detail
description: User taps any activity in the feed to navigate to its full detail screen. Cards show sport icon, name, key metrics, and 3D terrain preview thumbnail.

title: Load startup data in single FFI call
description: Home screen data (insights, summary card, preview tracks) loads from one consolidated FFI call. Eliminates waterfall requests and reduces startup latency below 100ms.

title: View form zone indicator
description: User sees their current training form zone (fresh, optimal, grey, high-risk, transition) colour-coded in the summary card. Provides instant readiness assessment.

title: View mini form chart sparkline
description: User sees a 30-day fitness/fatigue/form sparkline in the summary card. Scrubbing reveals daily values for each metric in context.

title: View activity map with GPS trace
description: User sees their recorded route on an interactive map with the full GPS trace rendered. Map supports pan, zoom, and style switching between light, dark, and satellite.

title: View synchronized charts
description: User scrubs through heart rate, power, pace, elevation, and cadence charts with a shared crosshair. Chart selection highlights the corresponding position on the map in real-time.

title: View activity statistics
description: User sees computed stats including TSS, intensity factor, normalized power, average HR, and training load. Six specialized hooks aggregate metrics from multiple data sources.

title: View HR and power zone distributions
description: User sees time-in-zone bar charts for heart rate and power on the activity detail screen. Zone colours match their configured zone boundaries for consistency.

title: View activity sections with performance
description: User sees all sections traversed during an activity with inline performance scatter plots. Each section shows best time, visit count, and trend direction.

title: View interval breakdown table
description: User sees structured intervals (laps, splits) with duration, power, HR, and cadence per interval. Helps analyse workout execution against planned targets.

title: View exercise sets and reps
description: User sees a grouped table of exercises with sets, reps, weight, and duration for strength activities. Weight displays in kg or lbs based on unit preference.

title: View muscle activation diagram
description: User sees front and back body diagrams with muscles highlighted by primary and secondary activation. Tapping a muscle shows detailed exercise breakdown for that group.

title: View section inline performance plot
description: User sees a mini scatter chart for each section showing pace/time progression over all traversals. Best records and trend direction are visible without navigating away.

title: Switch to 3D terrain map
description: User toggles between 2D and 3D map views on the activity detail screen. 3D mode shows terrain exaggeration with the route draped over real elevation data.

title: Export activity as GPX
description: User exports any activity as a GPX file for sharing or importing into other platforms. The export captures the full GPS trace with elevation data.

title: Create section from activity map
description: User taps start and end points on the activity map to define a new custom section. The section is saved to the engine and immediately available for performance tracking.

title: View wellness context for activity
description: User sees their HRV, resting HR, and form zone for the day the activity was recorded. Provides physiological context for interpreting performance data.

title: Progressive activity data loading
description: Activity detail loads progressively: metadata first, then streams, sections, and charts. Each stage renders independently so the user sees content within 300ms.

title: View fullscreen rotatable chart
description: User expands any chart to fullscreen with landscape support for detailed analysis. Gesture handling allows precise scrubbing across the full time range.

title: Browse detected sections list
description: User sees automatically detected frequently-traversed sections sorted by activity count. Each section shows distance, sport type, visit count, and trend indicator.

title: View section performance history
description: User sees a scatter plot of all efforts on a section with pace/time over date. Best records, median performance, and trend lines reveal progression patterns.

title: View section detail with map
description: User sees the section polyline on a map with stats cards showing distance, elevation, gradient, and record times. Map defers loading until after navigation animation completes.

title: Rename a section
description: User taps the section name to edit it inline with a text input. Custom names persist in the Rust engine and override auto-generated names everywhere.

title: Delete a custom section
description: User swipes to delete a section they created, with a confirmation alert. Frequent (auto-detected) sections can be dismissed but not permanently deleted.

title: Trim section boundaries
description: User adjusts section start and end points with a dual-handle slider on the section detail map. Supports both trimming (shorter) and expanding (longer via activity context).

title: Expand section bounds from activity
description: User switches to expand mode to extend section boundaries using the surrounding activity GPS track. A padded window (~500m each side) loads from the representative activity.

title: Detect sections with consensus polyline
description: Engine builds consensus polylines from all traversals using weighted centroids of nearby GPS points. Confidence and observation count quantify detection reliability.

title: View route group detail
description: User sees a route group aggregating multiple similar activities with a consensus route on the map. Performance scatter plot shows progression across all matched activities.

title: Create section from map taps
description: User taps start and end points on an activity map to create a custom section. Distance, point count, and a preview polyline update as the user defines bounds.

title: Load section traces in batch
description: Section detail loads GPS traces for all traversals in a single batch FFI call. Reduces from 474+ individual calls to ~3 calls for sections with 237+ activities.

title: View section direction statistics
description: User sees forward and reverse performance statistics separately for bidirectional sections. Best records and median times are computed per direction.

title: View section time range filter
description: User filters section performance data by time range (all time, 1 year, 6 months, 3 months). Reveals recent trends without noise from older, less relevant efforts.

title: View ranked daily insights
description: User sees prioritized insights generated from cached training data: PRs, milestones, trends, and patterns. Insights refresh each session and are framed positively.

title: View insight detail sheet
description: User taps any insight card to see a bottom sheet with full explanation, supporting data points, and navigation. Methodology section reveals how the insight was computed.

title: View insight methodology
description: User expands a collapsible section to see the formula, algorithm description, and source activities behind any insight. Builds trust through computational transparency.

title: Detect section PRs
description: Engine identifies personal records set within the last 7 days across the top-ranked sections. PR insights are the highest priority and surface Veloq's unique section tracking.

title: Detect stale PR opportunities
description: Engine flags sections where the user's fitness has improved since their PR was set. Surfaces curiosity about beatable records without prescriptive coaching advice.

title: Detect fitness milestones
description: Engine detects FTP improvements, pace improvements, and peak CTL achievements. Milestones provide concrete evidence of physiological adaptation over time.

title: View period comparison insights
description: Engine compares current week's volume, duration, TSS, and distance against the previous week. Changes above 10% are surfaced with directional trend indicators.

title: Detect activity patterns
description: Engine uses k-means clustering to identify typical training patterns by day of week and sport. Patterns with 60%+ confidence surface predicted activity for today.

title: Detect section cluster trends
description: Engine groups sections by sport and trend direction to surface aggregate improvement or decline. Two or more sections sharing a trend generate a cluster insight.

title: Detect aerobic efficiency trends
description: Engine analyses HR/pace ratio regression on frequently-visited sections to detect improving aerobic efficiency. A declining ratio indicates physiological adaptation.

title: Detect strength balance issues
description: Engine compares weighted set counts across antagonist muscle pairs over 4 weeks. Imbalanced pairs (>2x ratio) or one-sided loading are flagged for awareness.

title: Detect strength progression
description: Engine tracks weekly weighted set volume per muscle group over 4 weeks. Muscles with 15%+ volume increase are surfaced as progression insights.

title: View TSB form insight
description: User sees their current form zone with context about what it means for training readiness. Five zones (high-risk to transition) are explained with positive framing.

title: View training consistency insight
description: Engine detects streaks of 3+ activities per week sustained for 2+ consecutive weeks. Streak insights acknowledge discipline without creating pressure.

title: View activity pattern detail
description: User taps a pattern insight to see a typical-week bubble chart showing activity distribution across days. Highlights the predicted day and typical sport/duration.

title: View insight quick take summary
description: User sees a one-line contextual interpretation for each insight before opening the full detail. Provides immediate value without requiring a tap.

title: Compute insights under 100ms
description: Insight generation is pure TypeScript operating on pre-cached FFI data with no new queries. The entire pipeline runs in under 100ms to avoid blocking tab transitions.

title: See new-insights notification dot
description: A 6px orange dot appears on the Insights tab icon when new insights are available since last visit. Dot clears when the user views the Insights tab.

title: View CTL/ATL/TSB fitness chart
description: User sees a multi-series chart showing Chronic Training Load, Acute Training Load, and Training Stress Balance over time. Shared crosshair reveals daily values for all three metrics.

title: View form zone chart
description: User sees a colour-coded form zone chart mapping TSB values to five training readiness zones. Provides an intuitive visual of when the athlete is fresh, tired, or overtrained.

title: View power curve
description: User sees a power-duration curve showing best efforts at each duration for cycling activities. Supports time range filtering to compare current fitness against historical bests.

title: View pace curve
description: User sees a pace-distance curve showing best efforts for running and swimming activities. Sport-specific formatting (min/km for running, min/100m for swimming) applied automatically.

title: View zone distribution
description: User sees time-in-zone bar charts for heart rate and power with percentage labels. Reveals training polarisation and whether the athlete spends appropriate time in each zone.

title: View FTP trend over time
description: User sees their FTP progression plotted over months or years with detected threshold changes. Highlights periods of fitness gain and detraining for long-term perspective.

title: View decoupling chart
description: User sees aerobic decoupling analysis showing HR drift relative to power or pace. Lower decoupling indicates better aerobic fitness and endurance capacity.

title: View season bests
description: User sees their best performances for the current season across key metrics. Provides context for how current fitness compares to peak periods this year.

title: Filter fitness by time range
description: User selects from 7d, 1m, 3m, 6m, and 1y time ranges to focus the fitness dashboard. All charts update simultaneously to the selected period.

title: Filter fitness by sport type
description: User switches between cycling, running, and swimming to see sport-specific fitness metrics. Primary sport preference persists across sessions.

title: View activity dots on fitness chart
description: User sees activity dots overlaid on the fitness chart showing when training sessions occurred. Dot size or colour can encode training load for pattern recognition.

title: View HRV and resting HR trends
description: User sees HRV and resting heart rate charted over time on the wellness dashboard. Trend arrows and colour coding indicate whether values are improving or declining.

title: View sleep duration trends
description: User sees sleep duration trends with current vs previous period comparison. Sleep hours are formatted to one decimal place for easy scanning.

title: View muscle heat map
description: User sees front and back body diagrams with muscles coloured by training volume intensity. A 5-step colour ramp from light to saturated shows relative loading across muscle groups.

title: View muscle volume by period
description: User selects 7D, 4W, 3M, or 6M period to see how muscle training volume has changed. Period switching reveals whether volume is growing, stable, or declining.

title: View strength balance pairs
description: User sees bilateral muscle pair comparisons (quads/hamstrings, chest/back, biceps/triceps) with ratio indicators. Status labels (balanced, watch, imbalanced, one-sided) provide clear assessment.

title: View strength progression chart
description: User sees weekly weighted-set volume trends per muscle group over 4 weeks. Trend classification (up >15%, down <-15%, flat) summarises direction at a glance.

title: Tap muscle for exercise detail
description: User taps any muscle on the body diagram to see which exercises contributed to its volume. Lists exercises with set counts, rep ranges, and total weight lifted.

title: View muscle loupe on long-press
description: User long-presses on the body diagram to see a magnified loupe showing fine muscle detail. The 2.5x zoom follows the finger position for precise muscle identification.

title: Detect muscle hit regions
description: Tap detection maps screen coordinates to specific muscle polygons using pre-computed SVG hit regions. Ensures accurate muscle selection even on small mobile screens.

title: View per-activity muscle activation
description: User sees which muscles were activated in a specific strength training activity. Primary and secondary muscle involvement is distinguished by colour intensity.

title: Explore activities on regional map
description: User sees all cached activities plotted on a regional map with sport-type coloured markers. Tapping a marker shows an activity popup with key metrics and navigation to detail.

title: View route heatmap overlay
description: User enables a heatmap layer showing frequently-traversed areas with intensity colouring. Tiles are generated in Rust on a background thread and served as local PNGs.

title: View 3D terrain map
description: User toggles to a 3D WebView-based map with terrain exaggeration and real elevation data. Routes drape over terrain for an immersive view of climbing and descending.

title: Filter map by date range
description: User drags a timeline slider to filter which activities appear on the map by date range. Debounced filtering prevents excessive re-renders during rapid scrubbing.

title: Filter map by activity type
description: User selects which sport types to display on the regional map. Filtering applies to both markers and heatmap overlay simultaneously.

title: Switch map style
description: User cycles through light, dark, and satellite map styles. Each activity type can have an independent default map style configured in settings.

title: View section overlays on activity map
description: User sees detected sections overlaid on the activity map with numbered markers and coloured polylines. PR sections are distinguished with trophy markers for quick recognition.

title: Create section from map
description: User enters section creation mode on the activity map to define custom sections by tapping start and end points. Real-time distance and point count update as bounds are placed.

title: Spatial filtering with R-tree
description: Map queries use Rust R-tree spatial indexing to filter activities by viewport bounds. Only activities visible in the current view are loaded, keeping rendering fast.

title: View route and section popups
description: User taps routes or sections on the regional map to see popup cards with distance, visit count, and navigation links. Cluster popups expand to show individual activities.

title: View activity trace on map scrub
description: User scrubs through the chart on an activity detail screen and sees a highlight marker update on the map in real-time. Provides spatial context for every data point.

title: View 3D terrain in activity detail
description: User sees the activity route rendered on 3D terrain in the activity detail hero section. Camera pitch, bearing, and zoom are adjustable and can be saved per activity.

title: Receive post-activity insight notification
description: User receives a local notification with a relevant insight after completing and syncing a new activity. Notification content is selected based on the most impactful insight.

title: Configure notification categories
description: User toggles which insight categories trigger notifications (PRs, milestones, efficiency, strength, patterns). Individual category control prevents notification fatigue.

title: Register push token with server
description: User's Expo push token is registered with auth.veloq.fit after explicit opt-in. The token is associated with their athlete ID and platform for targeted delivery.

title: Request notification permission
description: App requests OS notification permission only when the user explicitly enables notifications. Permission status is synced with the toggle state on each app launch.

title: Deep link from notification to insight
description: User taps a notification to navigate directly to the relevant insight, section, or activity. Deep linking data is embedded in the notification payload for precise routing.

title: Fetch activity data in background
description: Background task downloads and ingests the new activity's GPS data into the Rust engine before computing insights. A 15-second timeout prevents the task from blocking indefinitely.

title: Deduplicate notification content
description: Background task compares insight fingerprints against the last-sent fingerprint to avoid duplicate notifications. Only insights with genuinely new content trigger a notification.

title: Notifications require OAuth
description: Push notifications are only available to OAuth-authenticated users, not API key or demo mode. This gate ensures webhook integration works end-to-end.

title: Switch theme mode
description: User selects light, dark, or system-follow theme for the entire app. Theme preference persists in AsyncStorage and applies immediately without restart.

title: Change display language
description: User selects from 17 supported languages with regional variants. Language preference updates all UI text, date formatting, and store metadata immediately.

title: Switch metric or imperial units
description: User toggles between metric (km, kg, m/s) and imperial (mi, lbs, mph) units globally. All distance, speed, pace, elevation, and weight displays update throughout the app.

title: Configure summary card metrics
description: User chooses their hero metric and supporting metrics for the home screen summary card. Sparkline toggle and metric customization persist across sessions.

title: Set map style per activity type
description: User configures default map styles for different activity types. Cycling might use satellite while running uses light for optimal readability.

title: View and clear data cache
description: User sees cache sizes for activities, GPS tracks, sections, and heatmap tiles. Individual clear buttons and a clear-all option free storage space.

title: Backup and restore preferences
description: User exports all custom sections, names, and preferences as a .veloq JSON file. Importing restores settings on a new device or after a reinstall.

title: Manage data sources
description: User sees which data sources are connected and can configure sync behaviour. Data source settings control what information is fetched from intervals.icu.

title: View account and profile
description: User sees their athlete profile image, name, and authentication method. Account section provides logout and session management controls.

title: View storage statistics
description: User sees detailed breakdown of storage used by the app including GPS tracks, terrain previews, and tile cache. Helps identify what is consuming device storage.

title: Manage tile cache
description: User sees offline map tile cache size and can clear cached tiles. Tile caching enables offline map viewing for previously-visited areas.

title: Access support and feedback
description: User finds links to GitHub issues, privacy policy, and app version information. Support section provides clear paths for reporting bugs and requesting features.

title: Configure recording permissions
description: User can grant or manage recording-related permissions (location, notifications) from settings. Permission status is displayed with clear upgrade paths when needed.

title: Set primary sport preference
description: User selects their primary sport (cycling, running, swimming) which personalizes default views. Fitness charts, pace/power curves, and insights prioritize the selected sport.

title: Authenticate with OAuth
description: User signs in through intervals.icu OAuth flow with automatic token refresh. OAuth provides granular scopes including activity write permission for recording uploads.

title: Authenticate with API key
description: User enters their intervals.icu API key as an alternative to OAuth authentication. Basic auth encoding is applied automatically and the athlete ID is discovered via /athlete/me.

title: Explore demo mode
description: User can try the app without an account using realistic demo data fixtures. All features work identically to live mode with mock API responses.

title: Handle API rate limiting
description: HTTP client retries 429 responses with exponential backoff up to 3 attempts. Rate limiting for bulk operations uses an adaptive limiter at 120 req/10s.

title: Handle session expiry
description: When OAuth tokens expire, the client triggers a session expiry flow that clears credentials and redirects to login. Prevents cryptic errors from stale authentication.

title: Retry network errors
description: HTTP client automatically retries on network failures (ECONNABORTED, ETIMEDOUT, ERR_NETWORK) with 2-second base backoff. Up to 3 retries before surfacing the error.

title: Call Rust FFI functions
description: TypeScript wrapper provides type-safe access to ~78 Rust FFI functions via UniFFI. Lazy loading prevents bundler errors in web and Expo Go environments.

title: Sync GPS data to Rust engine
description: Orchestrator hook coordinates GPS track downloads from the API into the Rust SQLite engine. Supports both demo fixtures and live API fetching with progress tracking.

title: Support 17 languages
description: App UI is fully translated into 17 locales including regional variants. Translation keys are managed through react-i18next with JSON locale files.

title: Persist preferences offline
description: All user preferences are stored in AsyncStorage via 15+ Zustand stores. Preferences survive app restarts and are included in backup/restore.

title: Cache API responses offline
description: TanStack Query caches API responses locally with configurable stale times per data type. Immutable data (activity streams) uses infinite staleTime to prevent refetching.

title: Navigate with bottom tab bar
description: User navigates between 5 main screens (Home, Map, Fitness, Training, Wellness) via a bottom tab bar. Inactive tabs stay mounted but frozen for instant switching.

title: Protect routes with auth gate
description: Root layout checks authentication state and redirects unauthenticated users to the login screen. Demo mode bypasses the auth gate with mock credentials.

title: Format values safely
description: All formatting functions handle NaN, undefined, null, Infinity, and negative values gracefully. Users never see invalid data strings in the UI.

title: Store GPS data on filesystem
description: Large GPS tracks are stored as individual JSON files on the filesystem rather than AsyncStorage. Avoids Android's 6MB SQLite limit and 2MB CursorWindow limit.

title: Validate data with Zod schemas
description: Runtime validation with Zod schemas ensures external data (API responses, stored preferences) matches expected types. Safe parse functions return null on failure instead of throwing.

title: Debounce expensive map operations
description: Map viewport changes, timeline scrubbing, and activity filtering are debounced at 100ms. Prevents excessive re-renders and FFI calls during rapid user interaction.

title: Freeze inactive tab screens
description: enableFreeze(true) prevents inactive tab screens from re-rendering while in the background. Combined with refetchOnMount: false to eliminate unnecessary data refetches on tab switch.

title: Handle engine initialization failure
description: If the Rust engine fails to initialize, all FFI-dependent hooks return empty results rather than crashing. Error boundaries catch rendering failures in map and chart components.

title: Deep link to settings sections
description: Settings screen supports scroll-to-anchor deep linking for navigating directly to specific sections. Used by notifications and cache management flows.

title: Manage concurrent sync operations
description: Sync orchestrator uses refs and abort controllers to prevent duplicate concurrent syncs. State coordination ensures only one GPS download runs at a time.

title: Generate route names from geocoding
description: Route and section names are auto-generated using reverse geocoding via OpenStreetMap Nominatim. Dual-layer caching (memory + AsyncStorage) with ~500m precision keys.

title: Adaptive rate limiting for bulk sync
description: Bulk API operations use an adaptive rate limiter that stays within 120 req/10s sustained limit. Prevents 429 errors during initial sync of large activity histories.
